_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron)
_meta.note: VA local batch — evidence-finalize Norfolk Commonwealth's Attorney Ramin Fatehi + Portsmouth Mayor Shannon Glover + Portsmouth Commonwealth's Attorney Stephanie Morales (3 evidence_local scoring records) AND Portsmouth at-large councilmembers Kathryn 'Kitty' Bryant + Mark Hugel (2 roster_verified, all-null per sparse-data discipline). KEY EVIDENCE: (1) Fatehi — Soros-funded D progressive prosecutor (~$650K Justice & Public Safety / Democracy PAC funding 2021-2025), 2nd term 1/1/2026-12/31/2029 after Nov 2025 unopposed general (defeated Butler in June 2025 D primary). FJP coalition signatory of the Sept 2022 joint anti-Dobbs prosecution-declination statement: 'I will never prosecute a person who seeks or has an abortion that would have been legal under Roe.' Authored Norfolk's 2019 cash-bail-rejection policy as deputy. Frames crime as 'symptom of structural racism' (Appeal). Co-founded 2025-26 'FAFO' (Fight Against Federal Overreach) coalition with Larry Krasner to prosecute ICE/CBP agents under VA code. Post-March 2026 ODU terror attack: 'Americans care more about guns than children… the cult of gun absolutism' — stood by quote when given chance to walk back. Mandatory office-wide LGBTQ+ sensitivity training. Police union backed his 2025 primary challenger. NO counter-archetype evidence. (2) Glover — D mayor of Portsmouth, 2nd term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 (won Nov 2024 49.9% over VM Lucas-Burke); not on Nov 2026 ballot. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2 on Rivers Casino-revenue-funded real-estate tax rate cut $1.30→$1.23 + freeze on other rates (his deliberate tax-cut framing of casino windfall is the cleanest local-rubric fiscal-restraint signal of any Hampton Roads D mayor). HARD FALSE self_defense q0 — he voted NO on Portsmouth's Jan 14 2020 2A Constitutional City resolution as a councilman; it passed 4-3 over his opposition (he has not reversed that posture as mayor). HARD FALSE public_justice q3 — he has explicitly embraced public-health framing of violence, calling youth violence 'a public health crisis' at the Hampton Roads mayors' forum, and his Project Safe Neighborhood model centers trauma-trained healthcare and behavioral-health stakeholders alongside (not above) police. 'Faith in God' boilerplate on city bio page is single-vector and does not name Christ — christian_liberty q0 left null per skill discipline. Pride proclamation: Portsmouth held its first-ever LGBTQ Pride Festival in 2022 under Glover but NO documentary evidence of a Glover-signed proclamation or city-hall flag-raising located — biblical_marriage q4 left null (no scoreable cell). (3) Morales — D progressive prosecutor of Portsmouth, 3rd full term 1/1/2026-12/31/2029 (re-elected Nov 4 2025 ~55% over Chapman/Heretick). Confirmed FJP signatory of the June 24 2022 joint post-Dobbs statement of elected prosecutors pledging to 'refrain from prosecuting those who seek, provide, or support abortions' — sanctity_of_life q0 + q1 both FALSE. Public-cash-bail-reform advocate (co-signed national prosecutors' letter to Cuomo; platform: end mandatory minimums + cash bail + death penalty) — public_justice q1 FALSE. Platform commits to 'lower the overall rate of incarceration in favor of other means of achieving justice and safety' + 'materially reducing prosecution of nonviolent minor behavioral offenses' — public_justice q3 FALSE (decarceral/public-health framing). Indicted Officer Stephen Rankin (manslaughter conviction 2016 for Chapman killing) + Officer Jeremy Durocher; 'only prosecutor in the country to have successfully indicted more than 1 officer involved in an on-duty shooting' — public_justice q0 FALSE (no anti-defund advocacy; police union sued her; pro-police archetype not met). Publicly explained/supported VA red-flag (ERPO) law (WAVY video) + requested AG Miyares Operation Ceasefire — self_defense q0 FALSE. NO counter-archetype evidence. (4) Bryant — at-large Portsmouth councilmember, Independent, Episcopalian (Children & Family Ministry Director at Trinity Episcopal Portsmouth, TEC affirming-parish), term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. After ~18 months in office her record is rhetoric-heavy and vote-light on every rubric category; no Pride proclamation vote, no abortion-sanctuary vote, no 2A vote, no published quote on life/marriage/CRT/SOGI/ICE. Per SKILL sparse-data discipline mark roster_verified; do NOT infer against her from TEC affiliation alone. Fix party Unknown→I. (5) Hugel — at-large Portsmouth councilmember, Independent, retired US Navy Rear Admiral (32-yr career, Naval Sea Systems Command), term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 — ON Nov 2026 ballot (declared for re-election). Cross-cutting endorsement profile (FOP Gosport Lodge #20 + Chamber + MLK Jr. Leadership Steering Committee + IAFF firefighters); pro-school-funding (touts '$19M increase in school funding'). Soft fiscal restraint signal (exploring tax rate reduction / rebate after assessment spike) but no roll-call vote. Soft pro-police-staffing campaign rhetoric without anti-defund framing. Per SKILL sparse-data discipline mark roster_verified; fix party Unknown→I; set candidacy_status general_candidate. PORTSMOUTH 2A NOTE: the Portsmouth 2A Constitutional City resolution passed 4-3 on 1/14/2020 — Glover (then councilman) voted NO; Hugel and Bryant were not on council (took office 2023 and 2025 respectively), so they have no scoreable 2A vote — leave null. PORTSMOUTH PRIDE NOTE: city held first-ever LGBTQ Pride Festival in 2022 under Glover but no Pride proclamation vote located — no scoreable biblical_marriage cell for any Portsmouth councilmember on that basis. PORTSMOUTH SANCTUARY NOTE: Portsmouth on the May 2025 DHS sanctuary-jurisdictions list (later retracted); city spokesperson said city was 'researching' the listing rather than disavowing it. No personal Glover/Bryant/Hugel/Morales/Fatehi public statement on the sanctuary label located beyond Fatehi's FAFO posture (separately scored).

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a specific Christian church affiliation, denomination, or public profession of faith. Iranian-American by background (born Suffolk VA, raised in Tehran until age 6 then Hampton Roads from 1985); Father was Iranian neurosurgeon, mother American-born nurse. Do not conflate with the unrelated 'Ramin Parsa' Christian-testimony content circulating online — that is a different person.
profile.education: Norfolk Academy (1996); B.A. History, Yale (2000); J.D., Columbia Law School (2003); clerked for Justice Elizabeth B. Lacy, Supreme Court of Virginia.
profile.background: Born Suffolk, VA. Brief DC corporate practice post-clerkship, then Richmond public defender, then Norfolk Office of the Commonwealth's Attorney from ~2006 (left briefly, returned 2012). Deputy Commonwealth's Attorney under Greg Underwood — authored Norfolk's 2019 cash-bail-rejection policy and the marijuana-possession declination policy. Elected Norfolk Commonwealth's Attorney Nov 2021 (took office 1/1/2022); re-elected unopposed in Nov 4 2025 general after defeating challenger John Butler (~5 pts) in the June 24 2025 D primary — second term 1/1/2026-12/31/2029 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot). Soros-network funded: ~$650K total across 2021-2025 cycles from Justice & Public Safety PAC, Democracy PAC, and Real Justice PAC (Fox/NRA-ILA/VPAP). Endorsed by Real Justice PAC, NIAC Action, IAPAC. Member, Fair and Just Prosecution coalition. Co-founded 2025-26 'FAFO' (Fight Against Federal Overreach) coalition with Larry Krasner et al. Wife Mary Beth (Master Lecturer at Old Dominion University); sons Thomas and James (Washington Blade 3/4/2021 profile cites his 'LGBTQ sons' as shaping policy).

ramin-fatehi.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk Commonwealth's Attorney (elected, partisan D). Second term 1/1/2026-12/31/2029 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. HARD adverse sanctity_of_life q0+q1 evidence: Fatehi is a confirmed co-signatory of the Sept 2022 Fair and Just Prosecution joint statement of 85 elected prosecutors refusing to prosecute abortion-related conduct. Direct quote on 13NewsNow: 'As the Commonwealth's Attorney for the City of Norfolk, our chief minister of justice, I will never prosecute a person who seeks or has an abortion that would have been legal under Roe… what is right has not changed.' That is categorical declination of any future VA abortion restrictions — clears the rubric bar for FALSE on both the public-profession cell and the affirmative-CA-action cell. HARD adverse biblical_marriage q2 evidence: his office publicly committed to mandatory 'LGBTQ+ sensitivity' training for all prosecutors and explicitly markets itself as 'a welcome place for… members of the LGBTQ+ community' (fatehinorfolk.com/priorities) — adverse on the biological-sex/trans-policy cell. HARD adverse public_justice q1 evidence: as deputy CA under Greg Underwood he was primary author of Norfolk's 2019 policy 'rejecting cash bail as a tool for prosecution, instructing all Norfolk prosecutors to cease demanding cash bail'; his stated legislative priority is to 'lobby to end all cash bail in Virginia,' quoted 'Nobody should have to pay their way out of jail.' Affirmative cash-bail-reform advocacy — adverse to the rubric's pretrial-detention cell. HARD adverse public_justice q3 evidence: per The Appeal post-election coverage, Fatehi 'says crime is a symptom of structural racism' and his campaign site centers root-causes/public-health framing — literal embrace of the public-health-of-violence framing the rubric scores against. HARD adverse border_immigration q1 evidence: Fatehi co-founded the 2025-26 'FAFO' (Fight Against Federal Overreach) coalition with Larry Krasner and others, publicly vowing to prosecute federal ICE/CBP agents who violate VA law — quote: 'Just because you have a gun and a badge issued by a particular agency does not make you immune to prosecution for crimes under the Virginia code.' Active anti-ICE-cooperation posture; Norfolk on CIS sanctuary-jurisdictions map. HARD adverse self_defense q0 evidence: in 13NewsNow/NRA-ILA/Bearing Arms coverage of the March 2026 Old Dominion University terror attack, Fatehi blamed gun-rights advocacy rather than the foreign-born felon perpetrator: 'Americans care more about guns than children… until there is the political will to break the spell of the cult of gun absolutism, you will see more incidents like this.' When given a Fox News opportunity to walk back, he said: 'I absolutely stand by what I said. It is the truth, no matter how much the gun lobby wants to deny it.' Categorical opposition to the local 2A protection cell. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on biblical_marriage q0 (no natural-marriage statement) or q4 (no Pride-proclamation signing located — his is a CA office, not a council vote; pattern is LGBTQ-affirmative but no specific scoreable signed proclamation, leave null per skill discipline), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — out of CA's lane; no library-content prosecution or declination policy located), christian_liberty (any cell — no church affiliation, no profession of Christ, no public-square symbol/prayer advocacy, no pastor-conscience advocacy located), economic_stewardship (CA office has no BBA-style framing in scope; his legislative priorities include funding more prosecutors and public defenders, NOT a restraint posture, but this does not earn an affirmative TRUE on q2 either), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (his felon-voting-rights restoration advocacy is soft adverse signal but not a direct cell match — left null), public_justice q2 (restorative-justice diversion is offered but no restitution-first framing located) and q4 (no Romans-13 framing), refuse_state_overreach q0-q4 (FAFO is anti-federal-overreach NOT in the rubric's subsidiarity sense — he aggressively asserts local prosecutorial authority in service of progressive ends rather than refusing state COVID/ESG/lockdown directives), border_immigration q2 (no explicit declination policy on immigration-related state crimes located beyond FJP membership and FAFO posture), self_defense q1 (office prioritizes 'illegal gun possession' prosecutions; no pro-CCW or firearms-in-buildings protection located). Counter-archetype context: NONE — textbook Soros-network / Fair and Just Prosecution / Real Justice PAC progressive prosecutor, no breaks from pattern.

0.note: Categorical public anti-life public-profession in CA capacity: 'As the Commonwealth's Attorney for the City of Norfolk, our chief minister of justice, I will never prosecute a person who seeks or has an abortion that would have been legal under Roe.' Co-signatory of the Sept 2022 Fair and Just Prosecution joint statement of 85 elected prosecutors refusing to prosecute abortion-related conduct.

1.note: FJP joint-statement pre-commitment to non-prosecution is the inverse of affirmative pro-life CA action — categorical declination of any future VA abortion restrictions.

2.note: Office publicly committed to mandatory 'LGBTQ+ sensitivity' training for prosecutors; explicitly markets itself as 'a welcome place for… members of the LGBTQ+ community.' Adverse to biological-sex / trans-policy cell.

Affirmative cash-bail-reform advocacy — adverse to rubric's pretrial-detention cell.

profile.religion: Personal values listed as 'Faith in God, love of country and family' on the official city Mayor bio (boilerplate; no specific denomination, congregation, or pastor named). NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a named church home or affirmative public profession of Christ in office.
profile.education: B.S. Interdisciplinary Studies, Norfolk State University
profile.background: Born and raised in Erie, PA, one of six children raised by a single mother. U.S. Navy corpsman; transitioned to health-insurance career; founded Commonwealth Employee Benefit Solutions (2003). Portsmouth City Council 2018-2020 (one term). Elected Mayor of Portsmouth Nov 2, 2020 (six-way race, ~35%), sworn 1/1/2021. Re-elected Nov 5, 2024 with 49.9% over Vice Mayor Lisa Lucas-Burke (40.6%) and Harold Carothers III (9.1%) — second term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot). Officially nonpartisan; D-aligned. Married to Paula; has children. Council-agenda evidence confirms he is still seated and chairing meetings through at least Nov 25, 2026.

shannon-glover.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor of Portsmouth (officially nonpartisan, D-aligned). Second term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. HARD positive economic_stewardship q2 evidence: at the Rivers Casino two-year mark Glover publicly framed casino-revenue distribution as enabling Portsmouth to cut the real-estate tax rate from $1.30 to $1.23 and 'freeze other rates for residents,' with ~$5M of ~$20M annual casino take steered to tax reduction and $15M to schools (WTKR). The deliberate tax-cut framing of a casino revenue windfall — and the council action that implemented it — is the cleanest local-rubric fiscal-restraint signal for a Hampton Roads D mayor and clears the bar for TRUE on the rate-restraint cell. HARD adverse self_defense q0 evidence: as a councilman Glover voted NO on Portsmouth's Jan 14, 2020 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution — it passed 4-3 over his opposition (with Mayor Rowe, VM Lucas-Burke). He has not reversed that posture as mayor and went on record expressing concerns about Councilman Nathan Clark bringing an AR-15 into a council meeting. Categorical opposition to the local 2A protection cell. HARD adverse public_justice q3 evidence: at the Hampton Roads mayors' forum on youth violence (13NewsNow), Glover explicitly described youth violence as 'a public health crisis'; his Project Safe Neighborhood / Portsmouth United model centers 'trauma-trained healthcare professionals,' behavioral-health stakeholders, and clergy alongside (not above) police — textbook public-health-of-violence paradigm the rubric scores against. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement; Portsmouth has not taken up an abortion-related resolution under Glover), biblical_marriage q0/q2 (no marriage-definition or biological-sex statement), q4 (Portsmouth held its first-ever LGBTQ Pride Festival in 2022 during Glover's tenure but NO documentary evidence of a Glover-signed proclamation, City Hall Pride flag-raising, or council vote on Pride located — no scoreable cell; left null per skill discipline), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — mayor doesn't sit on Portsmouth Public Schools board; no statement located on school choice, parental notification, CRT/SOGI/library content), christian_liberty (any cell — single-vector 'faith in God' boilerplate on city bio is not a Captain-voice affirmative profession of Christ in office; no church affiliation surfaced; no public-square symbol/prayer/invocation advocacy or pastor-conscience protection located), economic_stewardship q4 (no specific federal-style BBA framing; q2 already captures the rate-cut win), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (no statements on voter ID, paper ballots, or Zuckerbucks; Portsmouth council does not legislate state election law), public_justice q0 (his public-safety rhetoric centers 'partnership' and the 'strong arm of the law to enforce and hold them accountable' but does not clear the high bar — no quoted rejection of 'defund,' and his Crime & Gun Violence Reduction Commission framing is the same public-health body that drives the q3 FALSE — so cannot earn affirmative TRUE on q0 either), q1/q2/q4 (no cash-bail / restitution-first / Romans-13 framing located), refuse_state_overreach (any cell — took office Jan 2021 post-peak COVID; no anti-state-mandate / ESG posture located), border_immigration q1/q2 (Portsmouth on the May 2025 DHS sanctuary-jurisdictions list and city spokesperson said city was 'researching' the listing rather than disavowing; no direct Glover quote on ICE cooperation located either way — left null), self_defense q1 (his concern about an AR-15 in council chambers is single-vector and not a vote on a CCW-restriction ordinance — q0 already captures the 2A posture; left null to avoid double-counting). Counter-archetype context: D mayor who EARNS a TRUE on the rate-cut cell via Rivers Casino windfall, on-archetype otherwise (NO on 2A sanctuary, public-health framing of violence, presided over Confederate-monument removal consensus vote). The two FALSEs and one TRUE are all quote-bearing and pass the SKILL's per-cell evidence bar.

2.note: At the Rivers Casino two-year mark Glover publicly framed casino-revenue distribution as enabling Portsmouth to cut the real-estate tax rate from $1.30 to $1.23 and 'freeze other rates for residents,' with ~$5M of ~$20M annual casino take steered to tax reduction and $15M to schools. Deliberate tax-cut framing + implementing council action clears the local-rubric rate-restraint cell.

3.note: At the Hampton Roads mayors' youth-violence forum Glover described youth violence as 'a public health crisis.' Project Safe Neighborhood / Portsmouth United model centers trauma-trained healthcare professionals, behavioral-health stakeholders, and clergy alongside (not above) police — textbook public-health-of-violence framing the rubric scores against.

0.note: As a Portsmouth councilman Glover voted NO on the Jan 14, 2020 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution — it passed 4-3 over his opposition (with Mayor Rowe, VM Lucas-Burke). He has not reversed that posture as mayor. Categorical opposition to the local 2A protection cell.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a specific church affiliation, denomination, or public profession of Christian faith. Bishop Jerome L. Williams of New Testament Church endorsed her 2025 campaign but that does not establish her own affiliation.
profile.education: I.C. Norcom High School, Portsmouth (2001); B.A. magna cum laude, Norfolk State University (2004; Miss NSU 2002-03); J.D., William & Mary Law School (2009)
profile.background: Portsmouth native. First elected Commonwealth's Attorney in a Feb 10, 2015 special election; re-elected Nov 2015, Nov 2017, Nov 2021, and Nov 4, 2025 (third full term, ~55% defeating Nathan Chapman 24% and Steve Heretick 21%). Current term 1/1/2026-12/31/2029 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Wife, mother of four. Affiliations: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority; The Links Inc.; founding member, Law Enforcement Leaders to Reduce Crime & Incarceration; national board, Local Progress; Fair and Just Prosecution coalition member; endorsed by Real Justice PAC, Color of Change PAC, Voting While Black.

stephanie-morales.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Portsmouth Commonwealth's Attorney (elected, partisan D). Third full term 1/1/2026-12/31/2029 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. HARD adverse sanctity_of_life q0+q1 evidence: Morales is a confirmed signatory of the June 24, 2022 Fair and Just Prosecution Joint Statement from Elected Prosecutors pledging to 'refrain from prosecuting those who seek, provide, or support abortions' (one of four Virginia CAs alongside Biberaj/Loudoun, Dehghani-Tafti/Arlington, Descano/Fairfax). Categorical pre-commitment to declination of any future VA abortion restrictions — clears bar for FALSE on both the public-profession cell and the affirmative-CA-action cell. HARD adverse public_justice q0 evidence: Morales indicted Officer Stephen Rankin and secured a manslaughter conviction in 2016 for the William Chapman II killing — she told the jury Rankin 'brought a gun into what is at worst a fist fight'; also indicted Officer Jeremy Durocher. She is 'the only prosecutor in the country to have successfully indicted more than 1 officer involved in an on-duty shooting' (Real Justice PAC endorsement page). The Portsmouth police union sued her; she has made no anti-defund quote and no affirmative pro-police-staffing advocacy — the rubric's HIGH BAR is not met; this is the inverse pattern. HARD adverse public_justice q1 evidence: Platform explicitly advocates 'appropriate reforms of cash bail to ensure that non-violent offenders are not being detained pre-trial based solely on inability to pay'; she co-signed a national prosecutors' letter to NY Gov. Cuomo urging bail reform; called on the Virginia General Assembly to 'end mandatory sentencing minimums, cash bail, and the death penalty.' Affirmative cash-bail-reform advocacy — adverse to the rubric's pretrial-detention cell. HARD adverse public_justice q3 evidence: Platform commits to 'lower the overall rate of incarceration in favor of other means of achieving justice and safety' and 'materially reducing prosecution of nonviolent minor behavioral offenses' — explicit public-health/decarceral framing the rubric scores against. HARD adverse self_defense q0 evidence: Morales has publicly explained and supported Virginia's red-flag (Extreme Risk Protective Order) law in WAVY/MSN video segments and is vocally pro-prosecution on gun violence — she requested AG Miyares's help launching Operation Ceasefire in Portsmouth in 2023. Active support for restrictive firearms enforcement; categorical opposition to the local 2A protection cell. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on biblical_marriage (any cell — no public statement on natural marriage, biological sex/trans policy, or Pride proclamations / LGBTQ-rally appearances in her CA capacity), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — out of CA's lane), christian_liberty (any cell — no church affiliation; generic 'free association and peaceful expression of dissenting views' platform line is First Amendment boilerplate, not Christian-liberty specific), economic_stewardship (out of CA's lane; no BBA framing), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice q2 (restorative-justice diversion offered but no restitution-first framing) and q4 (no Romans-13 framing), refuse_state_overreach (any cell — she met cooperatively with AG Miyares on Operation Ceasefire 2023 rather than refusing state coordination — neutral, not adverse, not affirmative), border_immigration q1/q2 (no explicit ICE-cooperation policy located; Portsmouth was on the May 2025 DHS sanctuary list and Portsmouth Sheriff [separate office] cancelled a Warrant Service Officer agreement with ICE — but no direct Morales quote located; her FJP coalition membership is soft adverse signal but not a scoreable cell — left null), self_defense q1 (no firearms-in-buildings or CCW-restriction policy located beyond her general red-flag-law support which q0 already captures; left null to avoid double-counting). Counter-archetype context: NONE — textbook FJP / Real Justice PAC / Local Progress progressive prosecutor. Her one establishment-cooperative move (meeting AG Miyares 2023 on Operation Ceasefire) was framed as collaboration, not a policy reversal.

Co-signatory of the June 24, 2022 FJP Joint Statement from Elected Prosecutors pledging to refrain from prosecuting those who seek, provide, or support abortions.

1.note: FJP pre-commitment to non-prosecution is the inverse of affirmative pro-life CA action — categorical declination of any future VA abortion restrictions.

0.note: Indicted Officer Stephen Rankin (manslaughter conviction 2016 for Chapman killing — 'brought a gun into what is at worst a fist fight') and Officer Jeremy Durocher. 'Only prosecutor in the country to have successfully indicted more than 1 officer involved in an on-duty shooting' (Real Justice PAC). Portsmouth police union sued her. No anti-defund quote and no affirmative pro-police-staffing advocacy located. HIGH BAR not met — inverse pattern.

Cuomo urging bail reform; called on the Virginia General Assembly to 'end mandatory sentencing minimums, cash bail, and the death penalty.' Affirmative cash-bail-reform advocacy.

profile.religion: Episcopalian — Director of Children & Family Ministry at Trinity Episcopal Church, Portsmouth (Diocese of Southern Virginia, TEC); a theologically progressive / LGBT-affirming mainline parish under Rev. Elizabeth F. Keeler. Affiliation is vocational/employment fact; not an affirmative Captain-voice profession of Christ in office.
profile.education: B.A. Journalism & Media Studies, Rutgers University (2003); M.S.Ed. with secondary-education licensure, Old Dominion University (2009)
profile.background: Olde Towne Portsmouth resident since ~2010. Former newspaper reporter; former classroom teacher and department head, Hampton City Schools; since 2021, Director of Children & Family Ministry at Trinity Episcopal Church, Portsmouth. Civic: President, Olde Towne Portsmouth Civic League; Portsmouth History Commission; Girl Scout leader; Daughters of the American Revolution; former CASA volunteer. Husband Hunter; three children, all in Portsmouth Public Schools. Elected Portsmouth City Council at-large Nov 5, 2024 (finished 2nd of 11 with ~12.1%, three at-large seats); sworn Jan 2025. Four-year term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Officially nonpartisan; ran on 'committed to maintaining political independence' framing — no D or R slate; no NRA/VCDL/Family Foundation endorsement; no Democratic Party formal endorsement. Best characterized as nonpartisan-Independent with center-left civic-progressive valence; party set to 'I'.

kathryn-bryant.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Portsmouth City Council at-large (officially nonpartisan, Independent — see profile background for endorsement-pattern analysis). Term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. NO QUOTE-BEARING POSITIONS LOCATED on any of the 30 LOCAL-rubric cells after ~18 months in office. Her stated platform centers 'collaborative leadership' / 'spaces for all of Portsmouth's children to feel safe' / 'pragmatic, practical decisions about bringing business to Portsmouth' — generic civic-operator framing. Per SKILL sparse-data discipline mark roster_verified and leave all q-cells null rather than inferring from TEC affiliation, from her unaffiliated independent posture, or from purple-heart imagery in endorsement social posts. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement; Portsmouth has not taken up an abortion-related resolution under her tenure), biblical_marriage (any cell — Trinity Episcopal Portsmouth is an LGBT-affirming TEC parish where she works as Children & Family Ministry Director, which is a soft-negative signal on q4 by employment context, BUT no recorded council vote on a Pride proclamation tied to her is documented in public sources; Portsmouth Pride VA is a private nonprofit not a city-council proclamation; left null per sparse-data discipline — do NOT infer against her from parish employment alone), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — former public-school teacher and public-school parent; no voucher/ESA advocacy on record; absence of pro-choice-in-education evidence is notable but not scorable; no Portsmouth library-content vote or statement on record), christian_liberty (any cell — her ministry-director vocational title does not clear the Captain-voice affirmative profession bar; no quoted public confession of Christ as governing authority; no public-square symbol/prayer/invocation advocacy; no pastor-conscience defense), economic_stewardship (any cell — 'pragmatic about business' rhetoric, no recorded budget-restraint roll-call vote), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice q0 (generic public-safety rhetoric only — does not clear the HIGH BAR; the city's standing 'Crime and Gun Violence Prevention Commission' is explicitly public-health-framed but she has not opposed it publicly, so q3 also null), q1/q2/q3/q4 (no statements on cash bail / restitution / public-health framing / Romans-13), refuse_state_overreach (any cell), border_immigration q1/q2 (Portsmouth on the May 2025 DHS sanctuary list and the city did not publicly disavow that listing the way Hampton did — but Bryant herself has made NO public statement on ICE cooperation or sanctuary status; left null per skill discipline rather than inferring from city posture), self_defense q0/q1 (the Portsmouth 2A Constitutional City resolution passed 4-3 on 1/14/2020 — long BEFORE her tenure; she has no recorded firearms vote). Recommend revisiting after the Portsmouth FY27 budget vote and any 2026 Pride-month council action — those will be her first scoreable cells. profile.education: B.S. Systems Engineering, U.S. Naval Academy (1977); M.S. Naval Architecture & Marine Engineering and M.S. Mechanical Engineering, MIT (1992)
profile.background: 32-year U.S. Navy career; retired as Rear Admiral; last active-duty post was Deputy Commander, Logistics/Maintenance/Industrial Operations at Naval Sea Systems Command, overseeing 26,000+ shipyard civil-service workers. Post-2009 Sr. VP at CACI International, leading the Norfolk office. Past National President, American Society of Naval Engineers. Prior Portsmouth civic service: School Board, EDA, Planning Commission, Crime & Gun Violence Reduction Commission. Married Luana 1979; two grown children who attended Portsmouth Public Schools; built first Portsmouth home 1986. Elected Portsmouth City Council at-large Nov 8, 2022 as Independent — top vote-getter at ~18.6% in 3-seat at-large field of 11. Four-year term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 — ON Nov 3, 2026 ballot (declared for re-election; incumbent Bill Moody not seeking re-election; Lisa Lucas-Burke also running). Cross-cutting endorsement profile: Hampton Roads Chamber of Commerce + Portsmouth Firefighters IAFF 539 + Fraternal Order of Police Gosport Lodge #20 + MLK Jr. Leadership Steering Committee (Black-led D-leaning civic group). Classic Hampton-Roads consensus-Independent profile; party set to 'I', candidacy_status set to general_candidate.
